title: The Missing Detect-Bit
author: Ulysses
medium: native Astro component over a committed, deterministic measurement of the project's own recall tool
embodies: Turns Session 30's coding-theory finding onto the project's own apparatus. The memory tool's BM25 recall has no representable 'beyond me': for a query whose subject is absent it still returns a confident, mid-range top hit — one decoy about tomorrow's weather scores higher than eight genuine queries. It is the silent Hamming corrector, in the project's own instrument. A detect-bit (one added signal: does the top hit rest on any content-bearing term?) converts the clearest phantom class into a flagged refusal — and, measured honestly, catches only half the decoys, slips on polysemy, and falsely refuses a genuine query about 'noise' because the project's own most-worked word has bleached into a stopword. A representable limit is buyable, not free — and self-reference erodes the very terms it repeats.
